hits.question: Where is the LLM router defined and how does it pick a provider per tier?
hits.answer: ModelRouter.call(tier=...) in packages/asil_core/asil_core/llm/router.py. Tier → provider map lives in get_settings().llm_tiers; default profile is `tight` which routes reasoning → claude-haiku, embedding → text-embedding-3-small, cheap → groq-llama. Hard rule: never call providers directly — always go through ModelRouter.

hits.question: How are LLM costs tracked per call?
hits.answer: PostgresLedger records {provider, tier, model, prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, cost_usd, latency_ms, session_id} per call. Aggregated by /dashboard/cost endpoint. Budget enforcement lives in ModelRouter.call() guard.
